blinded.name: Blinded
blinded.summary: A blinded creature cannot see, automatically fails sight-based checks, has disadvantage on its attacks, and is attacked with advantage.

charmed.name: Charmed
charmed.summary: A charmed creature cannot attack the charmer or target the charmer with harmful abilities or magical effects, and the charmer has advantage on social checks against it.

deafened.name: Deafened
deafened.summary: A deafened creature cannot hear and automatically fails hearing-based checks.

exhaustion.name: Exhaustion
exhaustion.summary: Exhaustion has six levels that stack from disadvantage on ability checks up to death.

frightened.name: Frightened
frightened.summary: A frightened creature has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls while the source of fear is in sight, and cannot willingly move closer to it.

grappled.name: Grappled
grappled.summary: A grappled creature's speed becomes 0, it cannot benefit from speed bonuses, and the condition ends if the grappler is incapacitated or the target is moved out of reach.

incapacitated.name: Incapacitated
incapacitated.summary: An incapacitated creature cannot take actions or reactions.

invisible.name: Invisible
invisible.summary: An invisible creature cannot be seen without magic or a special sense, is heavily obscured for hiding, is attacked with disadvantage, and attacks with advantage.

paralyzed.name: Paralyzed
paralyzed.summary: A paralyzed creature is incapacitated, cannot move or speak, fails Strength and Dexterity saves, is attacked with advantage, and melee hits from within 5 feet are critical hits.

petrified.name: Petrified
petrified.summary: A petrified creature becomes an inanimate substance, is incapacitated and unaware, fails Strength and Dexterity saves, has resistance to all damage, and is immune to poison and disease.

poisoned.name: Poisoned
poisoned.summary: A poisoned creature has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

prone.name: Prone
prone.summary: A prone creature must crawl or stand to end the condition, has disadvantage on attack rolls, and is easier to hit in melee but harder to hit at range.

restrained.name: Restrained
restrained.summary: A restrained creature's speed becomes 0, it attacks with disadvantage, attacks against it have advantage, and it has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

stunned.name: Stunned
stunned.summary: A stunned creature is incapacitated, cannot move, speaks only falteringly, fails Strength and Dexterity saves, and is attacked with advantage.

unconscious.name: Unconscious
unconscious.summary: An unconscious creature is incapacitated, cannot move or speak, drops what it holds, falls prone, fails Strength and Dexterity saves, and melee hits from within 5 feet are critical hits.
